title: Exception handling and propagation

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific source-linked draft lesson for Exception handling and propagation; independent technical, source, pedagogy, accessibility, and runnable-example review remains required. sources.locator: The Python Tutorial; Python 3.14; Tutorial chapters 7-8: input/output, files, JSON, exceptions, and cleanup; atomic topic: Exception handling and propagation
sources.claim: Python exceptions propagate through frames until a matching except clause; responsible boundaries catch specific failures they can recover from, translate, or enrich while preserving the original cause.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Python exceptions propagate through frames until a matching except clause; responsible boundaries catch specific failures they can recover from, translate, or enrich while preserving the original cause.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A configuration boundary catches JSONDecodeError and raises ConfigurationError from the original exception with the affected safe path. Your goal is to trace exception propagation and implement a narrow recovery policy. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Function contracts and returns; Unicode text and formatting; Tracebacks and error categories. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: Python exceptions propagate through frames until a matching except clause; responsible boundaries catch specific failures they can recover from, translate, or enrich while preserving the original cause. This model is applied through the declared Python: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: except Exception excludes some control exceptions but is still broad, finally always runs during unwinding, else separates success-only work, and handlers can accidentally mask a second failure. The learner must reject this documented misconception: A bare except around a whole function is robust because it prevents all crashes and lets execution continue.

includes: Catch specific exceptions at responsible boundaries and preserve causal context.
Trace exception propagation and implement a narrow recovery policy.
excludes: except Exception excludes some control exceptions but is still broad, finally always runs during unwinding, else separates success-only work, and handlers can accidentally mask a second failure.

outcomes: Trace exception propagation and implement a narrow recovery policy.

examples.title: Exception handling and propagation: direct language case
examples.situation: A configuration boundary catches JSONDecodeError and raises ConfigurationError from the original exception with the affected safe path.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Python: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: A configuration boundary catches JSONDecodeError and raises ConfigurationError from the original exception with the affected safe path.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.

Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Catch specific exceptions at a responsible boundary, keep the try block narrow, preserve causal context, and recover only when a valid next state exists.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Catch specific exceptions at a responsible boundary, keep the try block narrow, preserve causal context, and recover only when a valid next state exists.

examples.title: Exception handling and propagation: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: A narrow ValueError handler covers user parsing but lets an unrelated programming TypeError propagate to the application error policy.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: A narrow ValueError handler covers user parsing but lets an unrelated programming TypeError propagate to the application error policy.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: except Exception excludes some control exceptions but is still broad, finally always runs during unwinding, else separates success-only work, and handlers can accidentally mask a second failure.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: A bare except around a whole function is robust because it prevents all crashes and lets execution continue.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Trace exception propagation and implement a narrow recovery policy.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: A bare except around a whole function is robust because it prevents all crashes and lets execution continue.
misconceptions.correction: Catch specific exceptions at a responsible boundary, keep the try block narrow, preserve causal context, and recover only when a valid next state exists.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: A bare except around a whole function is robust because it prevents all crashes and lets execution continue.

analogy.example: a records office that escalates named failures, validates decoded forms, and returns borrowed resources on every exit can introduce Exception handling and propagation, after the canonical Python: Beginner to Intermediate rule is stated first.
usefulFor: exceptions, custom failure categories, files, JSON, and contexts
limitations: exception chaining, suppression, path resolution, encoding, partial writes, and protocol methods are software rules

recap.summary: You should now be able to trace exception propagation and implement a narrow recovery policy. Keep this boundary in view: except Exception excludes some control exceptions but is still broad, finally always runs during unwinding, else separates success-only work, and handlers can accidentally mask a second failure.

Why is this belief incorrect: A bare except around a whole function is robust because it prevents all crashes and lets execution continue.
